topic: How sound drives second-by-second engagement (SFX, music, VO)
caveat: SFX Engine retention figures (35%/42%) are single-vendor with unlinked studies (med). LUFS targets, TikTok/Kantar stats, VO pace bands are high/med and well-sourced.

findings.claim: Precise SFX timing can raise retention up to 35%; comedic/payoff effects land best 0.2-0.5s AFTER the beat.
Impact SFX: +2 to +5 frames after the on-screen event (~85-210ms), not the exact frame; gag build -10 to -30 frames before.
EQ-shape SFX out of the VO band: +3-6dB presence at 2-5kHz, high-pass below ~120Hz.
88% of TikTok users say sound is essential; 73% would stop-and-look for content with audio.
Master to -14 LUFS integrated, true-peak -1 dBTP (-1.5 YouTube); hotter gets crushed by platform normalization.
Fixed loudness hierarchy: VO > music > SFX-as-accent; music sits below VO.
60-90s shorts VO pace: ~170 WPM energetic band (130 calm, 150 conversational, 190-200 fast).
60s script = 100-150 words; hook front-loaded into the first ~2 seconds.
Silence/abrupt audio drops are pattern interrupts: drop to silence 0.3-0.8s before a reveal.
Every discrete visual event gets an audible correlate (whoosh on transition, click on UI, impact on landing).
Music energy maps the narrative arc; switch sections at narrative turns, energy rising into the payoff.

actionable_rules: Master to -14 LUFS integrated (accept -15 to -13); true-peak ceiling -1.0 dBTP.
Music ducked >= 6dB below VO during narration.
VO cadence 150-175 WPM; warn under 130 or over 190.
Hook line + audio accent within the first 2.0 seconds.
Impact SFX +2 to +5 frames after the visual event; payoff SFX +0.2-0.5s after the beat.
Every transition/reveal has a matched sound event within ±150ms.
Music drop-to-silence (0.3-0.8s) or section switch before each major reveal; one interrupt in the 25-35s window.
Accent SFX high-passed ~120Hz, presence 2-5kHz, true-peak 3-4dB under VO.
